title: rubus neoebudicus
intro: Rubus neoebudicus, also known as the Madeiran black raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family native to the island of Madeira in the Atlantic Ocean. The medicinal properties of Rubus neoebudicus include its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as gastrointestinal problems and respiratory infections. The health benefits of consuming Rubus neoebudicus include its potential to reduce the risk of certain types of cancer, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Rubus neoebudicus can be prepared for consumption through various herbal preparations, such as jams, jellies, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
